text.title: Service Layer Anti-patterns
text.summary: Failure modes: business logic in controllers, DB access in services, DTO drift, and async error swallowing.

antipattern.description: Business logic (hashing passwords, checking duplicates, sending emails) placed directly in Express route handlers.
antipattern.reason: Route handlers cannot be unit-tested without an HTTP server; logic cannot be reused from queue consumers or CLIs.

antipattern.description: Service methods importing and calling Prisma/Sequelize/Knex directly instead of through a repository interface.
antipattern.reason: Direct ORM usage in services couples business logic to the DB layer, preventing mock-based unit tests and storage swaps.

antipattern.description: Generic repository method findAll(filter) where callers compose unindexed filter predicates.
antipattern.reason: Runtime-composed filters produce full-table scans that only appear under production load; index coverage cannot be verified statically.

antipattern.description: Per-controller try/catch that wraps all errors in res.status(500).json(), swallowing domain error context.
antipattern.reason: Swallowing domain errors loses error code, stack trace, and structured detail; the central ErrorMapper must handle translation.

antipattern.description: Zod schema updated without regenerating the inferred TypeScript type (or vice versa), causing runtime/type divergence.
antipattern.reason: Schema and type out of sync means the compiler approves code that fails at runtime validation.

antipattern.description: Service class registered as singleton in the DI container while carrying request-scoped state (auth context, tenant ID).
antipattern.reason: Singleton services with mutable request-scoped fields leak state across concurrent requests.

rule.statement: Run dependency-cruiser and eslint-plugin-boundaries in CI to block controller→repository and service→framework imports automatically.
rule.rationale: Human review alone misses import violations in large PRs; automated tools enforce the contract on every change.

text.reference: CI script using dependency-cruiser to block cross-layer imports
